title: S100a4 as a marker of treatment with spironolactone, pioglitazone and metformin
abstract: The invention relates to the in vitro use of a ligand specifically binding to S100A4, such as antibody for identifying the response to treatment with spironolactone, pioglitazone and metformin, such as in PCOS patients. The invention further relates to an in vitro method for monitoring responsiveness of a subject to a treatment with spironolactone, pioglitazone and metformin, the method comprising: determining the concentration of S100A4 in a body sample obtained from the subject prior or at the onset of the treatment, determining the concentration of S100A4 in said body sample obtained from the subject during said treatment, wherein a decrease of S100A4 concentration compared to the concentration prior to or at the onset is indicative for an effective treatment.
claims: Claims 1. In vitro use of a ligand specifically binding to S100A4 for identifying the response to treatment with spironolactone, pioglitazone and metformin. 2. The use according to claim 1, wherein the responsiveness is measured in a body sample of an adolescent girl. 3. The use according to claim 2, wherein the adolescent girl is non-obese. 4. The use according to any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein said ligand is an antibody. 5. An in vitro method for monitoring responsiveness of a subject to a treatment with spironolactone, pioglitazone and metformin, the method comprising : determining the concentration of S100A4 in a body sample obtained from the subject prior or at the onset of the treatment, determining the concentration of S100A4 in said body sample obtained from the subject during said treatment, wherein a decrease of S100A4 concentration compared to the concentration prior to or at the onset is indicative for an effective treatment. 6. The method according to claim 5, or the use according to any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the treatment is a treatment for NAFLD. 7. The method according to claim 5, or the use according to any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the treatment is a treatment for PCOS. 8. The method according to claim 7, wherein the treatment is combined with an oral contraceptive. 9. The method according to any one of claim 5 to 8, or the use according to any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the body sample is plasma. 10. The method according to any one of claims 5 to 9, wherein the decrease in the concentration of S100A4 is a decrease of 10% or more, of 20% or more, of 40% or more, of 60% or more or of 80% or more. 11. The method according to any one of claims 5 to 10, wherein the step of determining the concentration of S100A4 is performed between 1 and 12 months after the onset of said treatment. 12. An in vitro method of selecting a polycystic ovary syndrome patient for treatment by spironolactone, pioglitazone and metformin, wherein the method comprises the steps of: (i) determining the concentration of S100A4 in a body sample obtained from the subject prior or at the onset of the treatment, and (ii) selecting said patient for said treatment when the determined concentration of S100A4 is 10% or more, 20% or more, 40% or more, 60% or more or 80% or more higher than the S100A4 concentration in a healthy control subject. 13. The method according to any one of claims 5 to 12, whereby the patient is an adolescent girl. 14. The method according to any one of claims 5 to 13, wherein the plasma level of S100A4 is measured by a selective ligand against S100A4. 15. The method according to any one of claims 5 to 14, the effect of treatment or the selection of patient suitable of treatment is assessed by determining the level of S100A4 and determining the level of one or more of fasting insulin, HOMA-IR, LDL-cholesterol, and HMW adiponectin.
